edges.summary: Depends on persistence_core via git repository.
Pubspec defines the package described in the README.
Analysis options apply to the package defined in pubspec.
SyncCoordinator uses SyncLogger for event tracing and error reporting.
OmniCoordinator relies on the sync infrastructure for remote propagation.
Uses sync infrastructure via Outbox for template persistence.
Provides a synchronized view of divination cases using sync patterns.
SyncRuntime schedules and executes periodic sync runs via SyncCoordinator.
Ports interfaces use data types defined in types.dart.
OmniCoordinator operates on entities implementing the OmniEntity contract.

layers.name: Domain Contracts
layers.description: Abstract ports and shared value types in core/lib/model that define the hexagonal boundary all gateways and the sync runtime depend on.

layers.name: Sync Runtime
layers.description: Core engine of the persistence_core package: the SyncCoordinator, SyncRuntime, and the public package barrel that orchestrate pull/push cycles against the port contracts.

layers.name: Configuration & Logging
layers.description: Cross-cutting infrastructure for the runtime: SyncConfiguration loading/persistence, the YAML file loader, and the SyncLogger used by all sync components.

layers.name: Drift Local Adapter
layers.description: Local SQL persistence gateway implementing the core ports on top of Drift, including the generated companion code.

layers.name: Firebase Remote Adapter
layers.description: Remote persistence gateway implementing the core ports against Firebase Realtime Database.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Unit and integration tests for the core sync runtime and both Drift and Firebase gateway packages.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Per-package pubspecs, analysis options, Flutter metadata, default sync configuration YAML, READMEs and CHANGELOGs that scaffold and document the three-subpackage workspace.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: xuan_storage is a modular Dart workspace implementing a local-first hybrid storage model. It consists of a core synchronization engine (persistence_core) and pluggable adapters for local SQLite persistence (drift) and remote cloud storage (firebase). The architecture follows hexagonal principles, decoupling the sync runtime from concrete storage implementations via abstract ports.

tour.title: Core Orchestration (Omni)
tour.description: The OmniCoordinator is the primary orchestrator for hybrid storage operations. It manages writes that involve both local DB updates and outbox enqueueing for remote propagation. It operates on entities implementing the OmniEntity contract and records lightweight OmniOp entries in the outbox to track pending changes.

tour.title: Drift Persistence
tour.description: The drift package provides local SQLite persistence using the Drift library. It implements the OutboxStore and SyncStateStore ports. This layer includes DAOs for divination cases, decision links, and other domain entities, utilizing TypeConverters for complex serialization (e.g., coordinates and timestamps) into the SQLite relational schema.

tour.title: Firebase/Remote Sync
tour.description: Synchronization with remote backends is handled via Firebase Firestore and Realtime Database adapters. The SyncCoordinator coordinates push and pull operations using cursor-based pagination (TimestampCursor for Firestore, RevisionCursor for RTDB). SyncConfigurationManager handles dynamic tuning of synchronization parameters like intervals and batch sizes.
